Card.title: Card
Card.description: Represents a card in the card game.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Card entity.

flavorText.description: Flavor text associated with the card.

manaCost.description: Mana cost to play the card.

cardType.description: Type of the card (e.g., creature, spell).

rarity.description: Rarity of the card (e.g., common, rare, epic).

imagePlaceholder.description: Placeholder for the card image (e.g., filename).

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

createdCards.description: References to Cards created by the User. (Relationship: User 1:N Card)

games.description: References to Games played by the User. (Relationship: User 1:N Game)

id.description: Unique identifier for the Game entity.

startTime.description: Timestamp of when the game started.

endTime.description: Timestamp of when the game ended.

winnerId.description: Reference to the User ID of the winner, if applicable.

gameState.description: Current state of the game (e.g., in progress, finished).

Match.title: Match
Match.description: Represents a match between two players.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Match entity.

winnerId.description: Reference to User ID of the winner, if applicable.

startTime.description: Timestamp of when the match started.

endTime.description: Timestamp of when the match ended.

ChatMessage.title: ChatMessage
ChatMessage.description: Represents a chat message within a game session.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChatMessage entity.

gameId.description: Reference to the Game the message belongs to. (Relationship: Game 1:N ChatMessage)

userId.description: Reference to the User who sent the message. (Relationship: User 1:N ChatMessage)

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the message was sent.

messageText.description: The text content of the chat message.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. The 'userId' parameter identifies the user's document. It is used for storing user-specific data and preferences.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores cards created by each user. The 'userId' parameter identifies the owner, ensuring that only the user can manage these cards. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for simplified security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user (card owner).

params.name: cardId
params.description: The unique identifier for the card.

definition.description: Stores match data. Contains 'player1Id' and 'player2Id' to define the participants. These Ids are duplicated on the chat messages for easy rule definition.

params.name: matchId
params.description: The unique identifier for the match.

definition.description: Stores chat messages for each match. Includes denormalized 'matchId', 'player1Id', and 'player2Id' from the parent 'match' document for authorization independence.

params.name: matchId
params.description: The unique identifier for the match.

params.name: chatMessageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the chat message.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore structure is designed to support the CardCrafter application, focusing on card creation, online matches, and user profiles. It emphasizes authorization independence by denormalizing necessary authorization data and employs structural segregation to ensure clear security postures for each collection. The online match feature is implemented using a `matches` collection and associated `chatMessages` subcollection, which stores real-time communication. User-generated cards are stored within user-specific subcollections to maintain ownership and simplify access control. The design adheres to the principles of Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) and DBAC (No Custom Claims), making security rules simple and debuggable. All list operations are secured through appropriate data segregation.
